stories.title: Solid-state material turns visible light into high-energy UV at sunlight intensity, expanding solar energy potential
stories.desc: Two cups of warm water don't make one cup of boiling water. But in the quantum world, multiple low-energy photons can combine to produce a single, higher-energy photon.

stories.title: Room-temperature device synchronizes distant laser spots into single coherent 'supermode'
stories.desc: Researchers have demonstrated a new way to make spatially separated lasers synchronize and act as a single coherent light source—without extreme conditions or complex materials.

stories.title: Modeling nuclear fusion at lightning speed
stories.desc: As we scour and scorch the Earth for deeper wells of energy, investors and government agencies are pouring billions into nuclear fusion research. The hope is that fusion may ultimately provide a virtually limitless source of clean energy.

stories.title: Scientists create optical skyrmions using a two-century-old light phenomenon
stories.desc: Nanyang Technological University, Singapore (NTU Singapore) scientists have used a classic optical phenomenon known as the Poisson spot to create stable patterns of light called optical skyrmions, which are tiny, swirling configurations in the properties of light—akin to the spikes of a hedgehog.

stories.title: Experiment upends beliefs on how electrons actually behave in warm dense matter
stories.desc: Researchers at European XFEL, Helmholtz-Zentrum Dresden-Rossendorf (HZDR), Rostock University and other collaborating institutions have used high-precision experiments to demonstrate that the most widely used models for the behavior of electrons in warm dense matter are inaccurate. Warm dense…

stories.title: A minimal model for how a cell takes shape from the inside
stories.desc: Researchers at the University of Twente and Utrecht University have packed rigid, rod-shaped particles into soft lipid containers the size of a living cell and watched the container and its contents reshape each other. The vesicle's form determines how the rods line up; the tightly packed rods, in…

stories.title: NASA’s Cold Atom Lab is creating one of the weirdest forms of matter in space
stories.desc: NASA’s upgraded Cold Atom Lab is turning the International Space Station into a frontier for quantum research, creating ultra-cold matter that behaves in astonishing ways. The experiments could unlock new discoveries about the universe while paving the way for powerful future technologies in space…

stories.title: Pathway to high-fidelity quantum computing identified
stories.desc: Researchers from the University of Sydney, working with IBM, have identified and quantified important factors limiting the performance of quantum computers and demonstrated ways to overcome their impact.

stories.title: Iceberg Scours Reveal Ancient Wind Patterns
stories.desc: Scour marks left by icebergs that ran aground thousands of years ago record the winds blowing off the Laurentide Ice Sheet.
